symptoms.symptom_name: Adrenergic surge — drenching diaphoresis, pallor, fine tremor, palpitations, hunger and anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Autonomic counter-regulatory response to falling glucose; a hallmark early feature in insulin-treated patients.
Overlaps with anxiety, but cannot account for the objective low glucose or falling consciousness.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neuroglycopenia — progressive confusion, drowsiness, inability to follow commands or swallow

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Neuroglycopenic dysfunction marks severe hypoglycaemia and airway risk; defines the emergency and precludes oral treatment.
Altered mentation and diplopia can occur in stroke, but hypoglycaemia is a reversible mimic that must be excluded/corrected first.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedside capillary blood glucose 1.8 mmol/L (patient home glucometer, self-obtained, unverified)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A critically low bedside glucose with concordant neuroglycopenic symptoms confirms hypoglycaemia; patient-obtainable so admissible in telehealth, but unverified home-device reading.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mealtime insulin taken, meal skipped due to nausea (insulin–carbohydrate mismatch)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Provides the mechanism and predicts continuing insulin action and rebound-hypoglycaemia risk.
Visual disturbance is a recognised neuroglycopenic feature; here it resolves the stroke concern once glucose is known to be low.
Isolated transient diplopia without any focal deficit, in the setting of confirmed hypoglycaemia, argues against a primary vascular event.
No unilateral weakness/facial droop/speech deficit on the clinician's exam reduces the likelihood of a vascular cause — a clinician-measured sign, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced conscious level on formal assessment (GCS 10) with sinus tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective depressed consciousness confirms severity and airway risk — a clinician measurement (GCS), not available over chat; the AI infers severity from the carer's report of unresponsiveness and inability to swallow.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Adrenergic + neuroglycopenic symptoms with a confirmed low bedside glucose and a clear precipitant (insulin with skipped meal) that resolves with glucose — a decisive cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat capillary blood glucose after treatment (and ongoing monitoring)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms response to rescue and detects rebound hypoglycaemia while short-acting insulin is still active; a home-glucometer action the carer can perform.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Ambulance/ED point-of-care and laboratory glucose, electrolytes and assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive confirmation, IV glucose if needed, airway protection and observation for rebound — not obtainable via telehealth.
Do not pursue a stroke pathway first — correct the documented hypoglycaemia; imaging is unnecessary unless deficits persist after glucose normalises.
